data.title: If a deal is reached with Iran, Trump will travel to Pakistan.
data.description: According to Arab media outlet Al Hadath, US President Trump will travel to Pakistan if an agreement is reached with Iran. (Jinshi)

data.title: Senator Warren Seeks Clarity on X Money's Stablecoin Plans
data.description:
U.S. Senator Elizabeth Warren has reached out to Elon Musk for details regarding X Money, a payment feature anticipated to be integrated into the X social media platform. According to Cointelegraph, Warren, a vocal critic of Musk and the cryptocurrency sector, expressed concerns in a letter about the potential risks X Money's stablecoin and cryptocurrency integrations could pose to the financial system and U.S. national security. She questioned whether the platform might issue its own stablecoin under a legal provision in the Guiding and Establishing National Innovation for US Stablecoins (GENIUS) Act, which permits private companies to issue stablecoins.

Warren highlighted that X Money's limited beta preview indicates it plans to offer a 6% interest on deposits and collaborate with Cross River Bank, which has faced enforcement action by the Federal Deposit Insurance Corporation (FDIC). She raised concerns about the potential risky investments or data monetization activities that X Money or Cross River might undertake to provide such yields, especially when the Federal Funds Rate is between 3.5% and 3.75%. Warren's inquiry could reflect a broader legislative pushback against private companies issuing stablecoins under the GENIUS regulatory framework, which allows tech firms and non-banks to issue US dollar-pegged tokens.

Warren also questioned whether potential X Money users are aware that their deposits would not be protected by FDIC insurance if the platform were to fail. In March, FDIC Chair Travis Hill clarified that stablecoin user deposits are not covered by FDIC insurance under the GENIUS Act. He stated that the Act specifies payment stablecoins are not "subject to deposit insurance" or guaranteed by the U.S. government. However, the legislation does not explicitly forbid stablecoin deposits from receiving pass-through insurance, which extends FDIC coverage to each customer of an eligible financial institution up to $250,000 in the event of a company failure. Hill noted that while the GENIUS Act does not strictly prohibit stablecoin companies from offering pass-through FDIC insurance to end users, doing so would be "inconsistent" with the broader regulatory framework.

data.title: U.S. President Trump Considers Pakistan Visit Following Iran Agreement
data.description: U.S. President Donald Trump is reportedly planning a visit to Pakistan if an agreement with Iran is reached. According to BlockBeats, this information was reported by the Arab media outlet Al Hadath on April 16. The potential visit underscores the diplomatic efforts aimed at addressing regional tensions.

data.title: State-Led Digital Identity Could Address $5 Trillion in U.S. Fraud
data.description: Tricia Gallagher suggests that implementing a state-led, user-controlled digital identity system could potentially address approximately $5 trillion in U.S. government fraud and improper payments. According to NS3.AI, this perspective was highlighted in a recent newsletter. Additionally, the newsletter noted developments in FDIC stablecoin rulemaking and the issuance of Hong Kong's first stablecoin licenses, which are of interest to institutional readers.

data.title: Nvidia Rises 3.80% as It Breaks Above Falling Channel
data.description: Nvidia's stock closed at $196.51 on April 14, marking a 3.80% increase after surpassing a falling channel that had been in place since late October. According to NS3.AI, this movement highlights a divergence in performance within the chip sector. While the broader semiconductor index, SOXX, has risen approximately 28% in 2026, Nvidia's gains have been more modest at around 4%, indicating that the sector overall is outperforming the company.

data.title: Delysium co-founder Xiankun Wu transferred 131,750 RAVE to CEX half an hour ago.
data.description: According to on-chain analyst Ember, Delysium co-founder Xiankun Wu's address transferred 131,750 RAVE tokens, worth $1.7 million, to Bitget half an hour ago. He obtained these RAVE tokens two months ago through unlocking distribution/airdrop, at which time they were worth only $46,000.

data.title: Bitcoin Core developers: We'd rather freeze 5.6 million BTC than let them fall into the hands of quantum hackers.
data.description: Bitcoin Core developer Jameson Lopp stated that he prefers to "freeze" approximately 5.6 million long-dormant Bitcoins from the network rather than allow them to be acquired by attackers, rather than risk a potential future quantum computing attack. These Bitcoins have not moved for over 10 years and may be permanently lost, valued at approximately $420 billion at current prices. If a future breakthrough in quantum computing leads to the cracking of the private keys of old addresses, these assets could be transferred again, causing significant market volatility and even a crisis of confidence. Although the community recently proposed BIP-361, this proposal is still in its early stages and is not a formally implemented solution, but rather a contingency plan to address "extreme risks." (CoinDesk)

data.title: Tether's Bitcoin reserve address withdrew 951 BTC from Bitfinex 15 minutes ago.
data.description: According to on-chain analyst Ember, USDT issuer Tether's Bitcoin reserve address withdrew 951 BTC ($70.47 million) from Bitfinex 15 minutes ago. This is likely BTC they purchased in the first quarter of this year. The BTC held in this wallet has been purchased with 15% of the company's profits since 2023, and they frequently withdraw BTC purchased in the previous quarter from Bitfinex a few days after the end of each quarter. Currently, Tether's Bitcoin reserve address holds 97,141 BTC ($7.2 billion), making it the fifth largest BTC wallet. Based on the price at the time of their withdrawal from Bitfinex, the average purchase price of these BTC was approximately $51,312, resulting in a current unrealized profit of $2.175 billion.

data.title: Bitcoin Mining Firms Shift Focus to AI for Revenue Growth
data.description:
Bitcoin mining companies, which have established billion-dollar enterprises, are expected to derive the majority of their revenue from artificial intelligence by the end of the year. According to Bloomberg, this shift signifies a significant transition for an industry originally centered around cryptocurrency. The move towards AI highlights the evolving landscape of technology and business strategies within these firms.

The transition from Bitcoin mining to AI-driven revenue streams underscores the adaptability of these companies in response to changing market dynamics. As the demand for AI technologies continues to rise, these firms are leveraging their existing infrastructure and expertise to capitalize on new opportunities. This strategic pivot not only reflects the growing importance of AI in various sectors but also the diminishing returns from traditional cryptocurrency mining operations. The shift is indicative of a broader trend where technology companies are diversifying their portfolios to include emerging technologies, ensuring sustainable growth and profitability in a rapidly evolving digital economy.

data.title: Europe's Luxury Brands Face Challenges Amid Middle East Conflict and Weak Sales
data.description: Concerns over declining sales and the ongoing conflict in the Middle East are impacting Europe's luxury brands. Wall Street Journal (Markets) posted on X, highlighting the challenges these brands face as geopolitical tensions and economic uncertainties weigh on consumer spending. The luxury sector, known for its resilience, is experiencing pressure as demand fluctuates in key markets. Analysts are closely monitoring the situation, noting that the combination of regional instability and shifting consumer preferences could have lasting effects on the industry. As brands navigate these challenges, strategic adjustments may be necessary to maintain their market position and appeal to a changing customer base.

data.title: State-Led Solutions Proposed for Digital Identity Systems
data.description:
Efforts to address the challenges of broken digital identity systems are gaining momentum, with proposals suggesting that solutions should be state-led and user-controlled. According to CoinDesk, Tricia Gallagher highlights the importance of government involvement in creating robust frameworks that empower individuals to manage their digital identities effectively. The current systems, which often lack security and privacy, require comprehensive reforms to ensure user autonomy and protection.

Gallagher emphasizes the need for a collaborative approach, where governments take the lead in establishing standards and regulations that prioritize user control. This approach aims to mitigate risks associated with identity theft and data breaches, which have become increasingly prevalent in the digital age. By implementing state-led initiatives, the goal is to create a secure environment where users can confidently manage their personal information without fear of exploitation or misuse. The focus on user-controlled systems underscores the importance of giving individuals the power to oversee their digital identities, fostering trust and transparency in the process.

data.title: Trader Wang Xiaoer partially took profits on Binance Life, with a single account gain exceeding $470,000.
data.description: According to GMGN data, trader Wang Xiaoer continued to profit from his "Binance Life" trading account. His single wallet account accumulated approximately 773.67 BNB in ​​profits, equivalent to about $478,700, representing a return of approximately 95.79%. He also reviewed his performance, stating that in early April he judged the market to be at a temporary bottom and diversified his portfolio across dozens of low-market-cap cryptocurrencies, but overall returns fell short of expectations. With the market rebound, his main profits came from a few strong performers.

data.title: Circle CEO Jeremy Allaire Named in Time's 2025 Influential People List
data.description: Circle CEO Jeremy Allaire has been recognized in Time magazine's 2025 list of the 100 most influential people. According to Foresight News, this acknowledgment highlights Allaire's significant impact in the financial technology sector.

data.title: UK Chancellor Warns of Global Security Risks Amid US-Israel Actions on Iran
data.description: UK Chancellor of the Exchequer Rachel Reeves has expressed concerns over global security, stating that the world remains as unsafe as before the recent US and Israel military actions against Iran. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the growing tensions between U.S. President Donald Trump's administration and its international allies. Reeves emphasized the need for diplomatic efforts to address the escalating situation, urging for a collaborative approach to ensure stability in the region. The remarks come amid heightened geopolitical uncertainties, with potential implications for international relations and security policies.

data.title: Bank of England's Green: Market's Reduced Rate Hike Bets Reasonable
data.description:
On April 15, Jin10 reported that Bank of England Monetary Policy Committee member Green stated that the market's reduction in rate hike bets is reasonable. Current pricing, which suggests two or fewer rate hikes this year, is considered 'broadly correct.' Previously, swap pricing had indicated more rate hikes due to the Bank of England's readiness to address inflation from the Iranian energy shock. Green noted that few expect four rate hikes, which was the peak pricing level, and the current adjustment is seen as appropriate.

Green warned that even if the war ends immediately, the UK might face 'waves of small inflation surges' in the coming months. While businesses have already felt the impact of rising energy prices, consumers will only experience the pressure after the price cap adjustment in June. Disruptions in fertilizer supply could lead to increased food prices in six months. She expressed concern that rising energy prices could prolong inflation. Despite significant slack in the labor market, making a repeat of the 2022 second-round effects unlikely, the Bank of England will need time to gather conclusive data. 'There won't be concrete evidence for several months, and by the time we see it, it will be too late,' she said.

data.title: Euro-Zone Economy Faces Challenges Amid Iran Conflict, Says ECB Official
data.description: The euro-zone economy is experiencing a shift beyond the European Central Bank's (ECB) base case scenario, moving towards a more challenging outcome due to the ongoing conflict in Iran, according to Joachim Nagel, a member of the ECB's Governing Council. Bloomberg posted on X that Nagel highlighted the potential economic repercussions stemming from the geopolitical tensions, which could impact the region's economic stability. The situation underscores the need for careful monitoring and potential policy adjustments by the ECB to address the evolving economic landscape.

data.title: ECB's Nagel: Inflation Expectations Stable but Subject to Change
data.description: The European Central Bank (ECB) Governing Council member Joachim Nagel has stated that while inflation expectations remain stable, they could still change. According to Jin10, Nagel emphasized the importance of monitoring inflation trends closely, as shifts in economic conditions could impact the current stability. He noted that the ECB is committed to ensuring price stability and will adjust its policies as necessary to address any potential changes in inflation dynamics. Nagel's comments highlight the ongoing vigilance required in managing inflation expectations amid evolving economic landscapes.

data.title: U.S. Consumers Report Worsening Financial Situation Amid Rising Prices
data.description: According to NS3.AI, a recent report from The Kobeissi Letter reveals that 54% of U.S. consumers feel their financial situation has deteriorated compared to a year ago, primarily due to rising prices. This marks a record high in consumer dissatisfaction. Additionally, The Kobeissi Letter highlights a shift in employment trends, noting that the minimum monthly job gains required to maintain steady unemployment have decreased to an average of -3,000 per month, down from approximately 250,000 in mid-2023.

data.title: Qatar warns: The full impact of a war with Iran will arrive in one or two months.
data.description: Qatari Finance Minister Al-Khuwari warned at the IMF Spring Meetings that a war with Iran would have a greater economic impact in the coming months if the Strait of Hormuz remains closed and trade restrictions continue. He stated that the current energy price surge is just "the tip of the iceberg," and that "the full impact will become apparent in a month or two, bringing a huge economic shock." The attack on Qatar's Ras Raffarin LNG facility in March, which supplies nearly one-fifth of the world's liquefied natural gas exports, has already triggered global gas shortages. Al-Khuwari said it would take about five years to restore exports, adding that helium exports, used in chip manufacturing, are also a concern. He pointed out that Qatar supplies about 30% of the world's helium. He also described the risk scenarios of energy shortages escalating into "countries unable to light up" and fertilizer shortages leading to a food crisis. (Jinshi)

data.title: Israel's March CPI Shows Mixed Inflation Trends
data.description: Israel's Central Bureau of Statistics reported that the country's Consumer Price Index (CPI) rose by 0.4% month-on-month in March, compared to a 0.2% increase in February. According to Jin10, the year-on-year CPI growth for March was 1.9%, slightly lower than the 2.0% recorded in February. These figures indicate a mixed trend in inflation, with a higher monthly increase but a slight decrease in annual growth.
